rationale: The citation chain is circular (Ries cites Blank, Blank cites Ries) which the response fails to flag, and it misses Frank Robinson (2001) as the actual originator of the MVP term. Real domain knowledge is present but the response fails to surface a critical known risk (circularity) that the prompt framing should have triggered.
rubric_evidence: The MVP concept was first articulated by Eric Ries in 'The Lean Startup' (2011), citing Steve Blank (2010) as the originator
Steve Blank, in 'The Startup Owner's Manual' (2012), credits Eric Ries with the original formulation
The concept therefore traces to Blank-Ries collaborative work in the late 2000s
